articles.title: Partnerships & Stakeholders: Science and Technology Community
articles.description: The role of global science, academia, research and innovation is enshrined in the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction where it is recognized for the catalytic, boundary-pushing and transformative potential it holds for addressing the dynamic drivers of disaster risk.

articles.title: Orbán's era was over in a flash and Hungary's next PM is a man in a hurry
articles.description: Péter Magyar and his Tisza party are wasting no time preparing for the transfer of power after their dramatic landslide victory.

articles.title: Regional initiative rolls out in North Macedonia to boost early warning systems and disaster resilience in the Western Balkans
articles.description: A three‑year regional initiative to boost disaster resilience in the Western Balkans is now being rolled out in North Macedonia, with support from the Government of Italy through AICS and implementation led by UNDRR and partners.

articles.title: Building disaster resilience in Barbados' tourism sector: Lessons from Atlantis Submarines
articles.description: In the tourism sector, where safety, reliability, and reputation are inseparable, Atlantis Submarines shows resilience isn’t a one-off task or static plan but a living system shaped by people, processes, and continuous learning.

articles.title: From Resilience Assessment to Action: Training Trainers to Strengthen Urban Resilience
articles.description: UNDRR GETI held a Training of Trainers in Incheon to strengthen urban resilience. 30 experts learned to use the Disaster Resilience Scorecard to help cities move from risk assessment to multi-sector action, supporting the MCR2030 initiative.

articles.title: Teaching resilience with the Stop Disasters game: a teacher's guide
articles.description: This Teacher’s Pack supports the use of the  Stop Disasters online game as a practical way to introduce disaster risk reduction (DRR) and resilience in the classroom.

articles.title: First International Day International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes: Earthquakes don’t kill people; poorly built structures do
articles.description: 29 April 2026 webinar marking the first International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes, bringing together experts to discuss seismic risk, resilient infrastructure, safer construction and risk-informed development in the region.

articles.title: Disaster & Development
articles.description: The Disaster & Development Journal is a publication for academia, policymakers, and professionals working in the fields of disaster management.

articles.title: PreventionWeb empowers students with disaster resilience knowledge
articles.description: Early and sustained engagement with DRR knowledge and opportunities is essential to nurturing future professionals who will carry forward the principles of risk-informed development and community safety.

articles.title: Leveraging assistive technology for inclusive disaster risk reduction and climate action
articles.description: This April, in observance of the International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes, ATscale and UNDRR will host a webinar on ‘Inclusive Resilience: Leveraging Assistive’

articles.title: From local voices to global action: Women leaders driving climate and disaster resilience
articles.description: Policy dialogue highlighting women leaders and grassroots groups driving climate and disaster resilience in Europe and Central Asia, fostering partnerships to advance disaster risk reduction.
